title: Monitor, Control, and Protect Communications at System Boundaries
description: Monitor, control, and protect communications (i.e., information transmitted or received by organizational systems) at the external boundaries and key internal boundaries of organizational systems.

checks.name: Windows Defender Firewall Enabled on All Profiles
checks.purpose: Verify Windows Defender Firewall is active on Domain, Private, and Public profiles to enforce boundary protection

checks.name: Firewall Default Inbound Action is Block
checks.purpose: Confirm the default inbound firewall action blocks unsolicited traffic on all profiles

checks.name: Windows Defender Firewall Logging Enabled
checks.purpose: Verify that firewall logging is configured to record dropped packets and allowed connections for monitoring

checks.name: Network Profile is Not Public
checks.purpose: Confirm the active network profile is Domain or Private, not Public, ensuring tighter boundary controls apply

checks.name: Windows Defender Firewall Enabled on All Profiles
checks.purpose: Verify Windows Defender Firewall is enabled on all profiles on the server to enforce boundary protection

checks.name: Firewall Default Inbound Action is Block
checks.purpose: Confirm the default inbound firewall action blocks unsolicited traffic on all profiles on the server

checks.name: Windows Defender Firewall Logging Enabled
checks.purpose: Verify firewall logging records dropped and allowed connections on the server for boundary monitoring

checks.name: Network Profile is Not Public
checks.purpose: Confirm the server's active network profile is Domain or Private for appropriate boundary enforcement

checks.name: Explicit Perimeter Inbound Block Rules Present
checks.purpose: Verify Windows Firewall advanced security has explicit inbound Block rules covering the server's network perimeter

checks.name: Host Firewall is Active
checks.purpose: Verify iptables, nftables, or firewalld is running and has rules loaded to enforce boundary controls

checks.name: Default INPUT Chain Policy is DROP or REJECT
checks.purpose: Confirm the iptables/nftables/ufw default policy denies inbound traffic not explicitly permitted

checks.name: Review Listening Ports
checks.purpose: Enumerate all listening TCP/UDP ports via ss to verify only authorized services are exposed at the boundary

checks.name: Host Firewall is Active
checks.purpose: Verify iptables, nftables, or firewalld is running and has rules loaded to enforce boundary controls

checks.name: Default INPUT Chain Policy is DROP or REJECT
checks.purpose: Confirm the iptables/nftables/ufw default policy denies inbound traffic not explicitly permitted

checks.name: Review Listening Ports
checks.purpose: Enumerate all listening TCP/UDP ports via ss to verify only authorized services are exposed at the boundary

remediation: Enable Windows Defender Firewall on all profiles and set the default inbound action to Block. Enable firewall logging. On Linux, activate iptables/nftables/firewalld and set the default INPUT policy to DROP. Review all listening services and close unauthorized ports.
